heading: Advanced Research Projects Agency—Energy
label: 16538 Advanced Research Projects Agency—Energy
notes: References in TextLevel II of the Executive Schedule, referred to in subsec. (g)(3)(A)(ii), is set out in , Government Organization and Employees.section 5313 of Title 5

(b). , substituted “development of transformative science and technology solutions to address the energy and environmental missions of the Department” for “development of energy technologies”.Pub. children.heading: ARPA-E
children.label: 1 ARPA-E
children.text: The term “ARPA–E” means the Advanced Research Projects Agency—Energy established by subsection (b).

children.heading: Director
children.label: 2 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of ARPA-E appointed under subsection (d).

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 3 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Energy Transformation Acceleration Fund established under subsection ()(1).o

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: There is established the Advanced Research Projects Agency—Energy within the Department to overcome the long-term and high-risk technological barriers in the development of transformative science and technology solutions to address the energy and environmental missions of the Department.

children.label: i
children.text: reduce imports of energy from foreign sources;

children.label: ii
children.text: reduce energy-related emissions, including greenhouse gases;

children.label: iii
children.text: improve the energy efficiency of all economic sectors;

children.label: iv
children.text: provide transformative solutions to improve the management, clean-up, and disposal of radioactive waste and spent nuclear fuel; and

children.label: v
children.text: improve the resilience, reliability, and security of infrastructure to produce, deliver, and store energy; and

children.label: B
children.text: to ensure that the United States maintains a technological lead in developing and deploying advanced energy technologies.

children.label: A
children.text: identifying and promoting revolutionary advances in fundamental and applied sciences;

children.label: B
children.text: translating scientific discoveries and cutting-edge inventions into technological innovations; and

children.label: C
children.text: accelerating transformational technological advances in areas that industry by itself is not likely to undertake because of technical and financial uncertainty.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: There shall be in the Department of Energy a Director of ARPA-E, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.heading: Qualifications
children.label: 2 Qualifications
children.text: The Director shall be an individual who, by reason of professional background and experience, is especially qualified to advise the Secretary on, and manage research programs addressing, matters pertaining to long-term and high-risk technological barriers to the development of energy technologies.

children.heading: Relationship to Secretary
children.label: 3 Relationship to Secretary
children.text: The Director shall report to the Secretary.

children.heading: Relationship to other programs
children.label: 4 Relationship to other programs
children.text: No other programs within the Department shall report to the Director.
approving all new programs within ARPA-E;
developing funding criteria and assessing the success of programs through the establishment of technical milestones;

children.label: A
children.text: novel early-stage research with possible technology applications;

children.label: B
children.text: development of techniques, processes, and technologies, and related testing and evaluation;

children.label: C
children.text: research and development of advanced manufacturing process and technologies for the domestic manufacturing of novel energy technologies; and

children.label: D
children.text: coordination with nongovernmental entities for demonstration of technologies and research applications to facilitate technology transfer;
terminating programs carried out under this section that are not achieving the goals of the programs; and

children.label: A
children.text: ensuring that applications for funding disclose the extent of current and prior efforts, including monetary investments as appropriate, in pursuit of the technology area for which funding is being requested;

children.label: B
children.text: adopting measures to ensure that, in making awards, program managers adhere to the purposes of subsection (c)(2)(C); and

children.label: C
children.text: providing as part of the annual report required by subsection (h)(1) a summary of the instances of and reasons for ARPA–E funding projects in technology areas already being undertaken by industry.

children.heading: Awards
children.label: f Awards
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Director may provide awards in the form of grants, contracts, cooperative agreements, cash prizes, and other transactions.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall establish and maintain within ARPA–E a staff with sufficient qualifications and expertise to enable ARPA–E to carry out the responsibilities of ARPA–E under this section in conjunction with other operations of the Department.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director shall designate employees to serve as program directors for the programs established pursuant to the responsibilities established for ARPA-E under subsection (e).

children.label: i
children.text: establishing research and development goals for the program, including through the convening of workshops and conferring with outside experts, and publicizing the goals of the program to the public and private sectors;

children.label: ii
children.text: soliciting applications for specific areas of particular promise, especially areas that the private sector or the Federal Government are not likely to undertake alone;

children.label: iii
children.text: building research collaborations for carrying out the program;

children.label: I
children.text: the novelty and scientific and technical merit of the proposed projects;

children.label: II
children.text: the demonstrated capabilities of the applicants to successfully carry out the proposed project;

children.label: III
children.text: the consideration by the applicant of future commercial applications of the project, including the feasibility of partnering with 1 or more commercial entities; and

children.label: IV
children.text: such other criteria as are established by the Director;

children.label: v
children.text: identifying innovative cost-sharing arrangements for ARPA–E projects, including through use of the authority provided under ;section 16352(b)(3) of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: monitoring the progress of projects supported under the program;

children.label: vii
children.text: identifying mechanisms for commercial application of successful energy technology development projects, including through establishment of partnerships between awardees and commercial entities; and

children.label: viii
children.text: recommending program restructure or termination of research partnerships or whole projects.

children.heading: Term
children.label: C Term
children.text: The term of a program manager shall be not more than 3 years and may be renewed.

children.label: i
children.text: make appointments of scientific, engineering, and professional personnel without regard to the civil service laws;

children.label: ii
children.text: fix the basic pay of such personnel at a rate to be determined by the Director at rates not in excess of Level II of the Executive Schedule (EX–II) without regard to the civil service laws; and

children.label: II
children.text: The amount equal to 25 percent of the annual rate of basic pay of the employee.

children.label: III
children.text: The amount of the limitation that is applicable for a calendar year under .section 5307(a)(1) of title 5

children.heading: Number
children.label: B Number
children.text: The Director shall appoint not more than 120 personnel under this section.

children.heading: Private recruiting firms
children.label: C Private recruiting firms
children.text: The Secretary, or the Director serving as an agent of the Secretary, may contract with private recruiting firms for the hiring of qualified technical staff to carry out this section.

children.heading: Additional staff
children.label: D Additional staff
children.text: The Director may use all authorities in existence on , that are provided to the Secretary to hire administrative, financial, and clerical staff as necessary to carry out this section.August 9, 2007

children.label: A
children.text: describes projects supported by ARPA–E during the previous fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: describes projects supported by ARPA–E during the previous fiscal year that examine topics and technologies closely related to other activities funded by the Department, and includes an analysis of whether in supporting such projects, the Director is in compliance with subsection (i)(1); and

children.label: C
children.text: describes current, proposed, and planned projects to be carried out pursuant to subsection (e)(3)(D).

children.heading: Strategic vision roadmap
children.label: 2 Strategic vision roadmap
children.text: Not later than , and every four years thereafter, the Director shall provide to the relevant authorizing and appropriations committees of Congress a roadmap describing the strategic vision that ARPA–E will use to guide the choices of ARPA–E for future technology investments over the following 4 fiscal years.October 1, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: the activities of ARPA–E are coordinated with, and do not duplicate the efforts of, programs and laboratories within the Department and other relevant research agencies; and

children.label: B
children.text: ARPA–E does not provide funding for a project unless the prospective grantee demonstrates sufficient attempts to secure private financing or indicates that the project is not independently commercially viable.

children.heading: Technology Transfer Coordinator
children.label: 2 Technology Transfer Coordinator
children.text: To the extent appropriate, the Director may coordinate technology transfer efforts with the Technology Transfer Coordinator appointed under .section 16391 of this title

children.heading: Federal demonstration of technologies
children.label: j Federal demonstration of technologies
children.text: The Director shall seek opportunities to partner with purchasing and procurement programs of Federal agencies to demonstrate energy technologies resulting from activities funded through ARPA–E.

children.label: A
children.text: an existing Department of Energy advisory committee; and

children.label: i
children.text: specific program tasks; or

children.label: ii
children.text: overall direction of ARPA-E.

children.label: A
children.text: the President’s Committee of Advisors on Science and Technology; and

children.label: B
children.text: any professional or scientific organization with expertise in specific processes or technologies under development by ARPA-E.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Secretary is authorized to enter into a contract with the National Academy of Sciences under which the National Academy shall conduct an evaluation of how well ARPA–E is achieving the goals and mission of ARPA–E.December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: a recommendation on whether ARPA-E should be continued or terminated; and

children.label: B
children.text: a description of lessons learned from operation of ARPA-E, and the manner in which those lessons may apply to the operation of other programs of the Department.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 3 Availability
children.text: On completion of the evaluation, the evaluation shall be made available to Congress and the public.
in addition to existing authorities granted to the Secretary; and
are not intended to supersede or modify any existing authorities.
Plans for commercialization of technologies developed under the award, including business plans, technology-to-market plans, market studies, and cost and performance models.

Investments provided to an awardee from third parties (such as venture capital firms, hedge funds, and private equity firms), including amounts and the percentage of ownership of the awardee provided in return for the investments.

children.label: A
children.text: plans to or has invested into the technology developed under the award; or

children.label: B
children.text: is seeking from third parties.
Revenue from the licensing or sale of new products or services resulting from research conducted under the award.

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 1 Fund
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a fund, to be known as the “Energy Transformation Acceleration Fund”, which shall be administered by the Director for the purposes of carrying out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: $435,000,000 for fiscal year 2021;

children.label: B
children.text: $500,000,000 for fiscal year 2022;

children.label: C
children.text: $575,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: D
children.text: $662,000,000 for fiscal year 2024; and

children.label: E
children.text: $761,000,000 for fiscal year 2025.

children.heading: Budget request
children.label: A Budget request
children.text: The budget request for ARPA-E shall be separate from the rest of the budget of the Department.

children.heading: Appropriations
children.label: B Appropriations
children.text: Appropriations to the Fund shall be separate and distinct from the rest of the budget for the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: not more than 50 percent of the amount shall be used to carry out subsection (e)(3)(D);

children.label: B
children.text: at least 5 percent of the amount shall be used for technology transfer and outreach activities, consistent with the goal described in subsection (c)(2)(C) and within the responsibilities of program directors described in subsection (g)(2)(B)(vii); and

children.label: C
children.text: no funds may be used for construction of new buildings or facilities during the 5-year period beginning on .August 9, 2007
